title: Xamarin/.NET MAUI: Cross-Platform UI Framework
description: Build iOS, Android, and Windows apps using Xamarin (legacy) or .NET MAUI (modern) with C# and .NET ecosystem; strong for enterprises with .NET investments.

cost.costNotes: Primary costs are development labor. Good for enterprises already using .NET.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: Client-side framework with no direct infrastructure costs. Min ($0) represents basic development with free Visual Studio Community and free app store accounts during development. Max ($100) represents production app store fees: Apple Developer Program ($99/year ≈ $8/mo) + Google Play ($25 one-time, amortized ≈ $2/mo) + optional Microsoft Partner Network benefits. Does not include optional Visual Studio Enterprise subscription ($250/mo per user) or CI/CD services (Azure DevOps free tier available, paid starts at $30-50/mo).
provenance.adoptionCost: High complexity due to: (1) Learning curve for .NET MAUI's new architecture if coming from Xamarin ($1000 - significant API changes, new project structure, handler-based rendering); (2) Platform-specific customization knowledge required for iOS/Android native features ($800 - handlers, platform effects, native embedding); (3) Cross-platform UI paradigm requires understanding layout differences and responsive design across mobile/desktop ($600); (4) Tooling setup and configuration across Windows/Mac build environments ($400); (5) Migration effort for existing Xamarin projects ($200). Lower than pure native development ($8000+) but higher than web-based cross-platform solutions like React Native ($1500-2000) due to smaller community and fewer learning resources. AI coding assistants moderately helpful but .NET MAUI-specific examples are less common than React/Flutter.
provenance.licenseCost: Framework is open source under MIT license with no licensing fees. Visual Studio Community edition is free for individuals and small teams (under 5 users, <$1M revenue). Apple/Google developer accounts ($99/year and $25 one-time respectively) are operational costs, not licenses. Optional Visual Studio Enterprise ($5,999/year per user) or Professional ($499/year per user) would add costs but are not required for .NET MAUI development. Core runtime and framework are completely free for commercial use.

provides.reasoning: Core capability of Xamarin/.NET MAUI - enables single codebase to generate native UIs across iOS, Android, and Windows platforms.
Primary use case is building native mobile applications for iOS and Android with shared UI code.
Xamarin/.NET MAUI is built on .NET runtime and provides full access to .NET libraries, NuGet packages, and tooling ecosystem.
.NET MAUI explicitly supports Windows desktop applications as a target platform alongside mobile platforms.
Xamarin/.NET MAUI compiles to native platform controls rather than web views, providing true native UI experiences and performance.
The framework abstracts platform-specific APIs through a unified interface, allowing developers to write cross-platform code while still accessing native features.

requires.reasoning: C# is the primary development language for Xamarin/.NET MAUI applications; developers must be proficient in C# language features and idioms.
Understanding of .NET runtime, BCL (Base Class Library), project structure, and tooling is essential for effective development.
XAML is the declarative markup language used for defining UI layouts in Xamarin/.NET MAUI; developers need proficiency in XAML syntax and data binding.
Developers need Visual Studio or VS Code with specific workloads, SDKs, and emulators installed to build and test Xamarin/.NET MAUI applications.
While the framework provides abstraction, developers often need understanding of iOS/Android platform concepts, permissions, and lifecycle for advanced scenarios and platform-specific features.

availability.reason: Xamarin/.NET MAUI are open source; documentation freely available.

supports_nfr.reason: Client-side framework availability limited by device issues, OS fragmentation, and app crashes beyond framework control.
Xamarin mobile apps require app store review for updates (iOS: 24-48hrs, Android: hours) constraining recovery time.
Pattern supports GDPR compliance through data handling controls, encryption, and user consent management in mobile apps.
Pattern can support HIPAA compliance with proper encryption, secure storage, and secure data transmission implementation.
Pattern supports CCPA compliance through user consent management and data access controls in client applications.
Pattern can support SOX compliance through audit trails and secure data handling in financial applications.

supports_constraints.reason: Xamarin/.NET MAUI is a client-side cross-platform UI framework that runs on devices independent of cloud infrastructure.
Xamarin and .NET MAUI are C#-only frameworks built exclusively on the .NET ecosystem.
Xamarin/.NET MAUI targets iOS and Android mobile platforms, plus Windows and macOS desktop applications.
Pattern supports local caching through .NET caching libraries and platform-specific cache mechanisms.
Pattern can integrate with async messaging through .NET client libraries for various message brokers and event systems.
Pattern supports AI/ML inference on device through .NET libraries like ML.NET and platform-specific ML frameworks.
Client applications can support multi-tenancy through user authentication and data isolation at the application level.
Pattern can implement batch processing for local data operations and background tasks using platform background services.
Client applications can participate in distributed transactions through backend service orchestration and coordination.
Pattern supports real-time streaming through SignalR, WebSockets, and platform-specific push notification services.
Pattern can integrate vector search through .NET client libraries connecting to backend vector databases.
Pattern can use document stores through .NET client libraries for MongoDB, CosmosDB, and local document databases.
Pattern can use local key-value stores like SQLite or platform preferences for device-local data storage.
Pattern can integrate graph databases through .NET client libraries for Neo4j and other graph database providers.
Pattern can integrate time-series databases through .NET client libraries for InfluxDB, TimescaleDB, and similar systems.
Client applications can support OLTP workloads through local database access and backend transactional service integration.
Client applications can support OLAP workloads through data visualization and backend analytics service integration.

framework_version.description: Xamarin.Forms is legacy (maintenance mode), .NET MAUI is the modern unified framework with better performance, hot reload, and single project structure. Choose Xamarin.Forms only for existing codebases.

ui_paradigm.description: XAML is declarative UI markup (most common), C# Markup is fluent code-based UI, Blazor Hybrid embeds Blazor web components in native shell. XAML offers best tooling, Blazor enables web skill reuse.

target_platforms.description: Target deployment platforms. iOS/Android are primary mobile targets, Windows/macOS for desktop. More platforms increase testing complexity but maximize reach.

native_interop.description: Strategy for accessing native APIs. Bindings wrap native libraries, Platform-specifics use conditional compilation, DependencyService uses DI for platform code, Custom Renderers override UI controls. Complexity increases left to right.

architecture_pattern.description: Application architecture pattern. MVVM (Model-View-ViewModel) is recommended with data binding, MVC/MVP are alternatives, Clean Architecture adds layered separation for large apps with complex business logic.
